notes: POWER RANKINGS DATA — single source of truth.
rankings-loader.js reads this file, renders into #power-rankings-mount.
Structure: { weeks: [ ... ] }
Each week has: id, label, subtitle, tag, rankings[]
- id: stable URL fragment-friendly slug ('wk-3'). Used for tab buttons.
- label: short tab label ('WK 3').
- subtitle: longer description shown in section header ('WEEK OF MAY 1, 2026').
- tag: small contextual label ('RACE WEEK', 'PRE-SEASON', etc.) shown next to subtitle.
- rankings: array of team entries (10 ideal, but can be fewer).
WEEKS ORDER: weeks[0] is the LATEST (current) week — renders by default.
Older weeks (weeks[1], weeks[2]...) are accessible via tabs.
Empty rankings[] array = stub week, shows 'rankings not yet archived' state.
Each ranking entry: { rank, previous_rank, ticker, team_name, color, logo, score, commentary, tweet }
- rank: integer position (1 = leader)
- previous_rank: integer (last week's rank). Used to compute movement arrow.
Set to null for new entries → renders 'NEW' badge.
- ticker: '$BRETT' (with $ prefix)
- team_name: full name ('BRETT IS BACK')
- color: hex color used for left rail + accent
- logo: filename in same dir ('brett.png'). Falls back to ticker badge if missing.
- score: float 0-100 (display only — logic to be defined)
- commentary: 1-line analyst note (~80 chars ideal)
- tweet: per-team copy-to-clipboard tweet text. \n for line breaks.
MOVEMENT ARROWS (auto-computed from previous_rank - rank):
positive  → ▲ green   ('+2', '+5')
negative  → ▼ red     ('-1', '-3')
zero      → —  chrome ('—')
null prev → NEW gold badge
TO ADD A NEW WEEK: prepend a new object to weeks[]. Done.
TO UPDATE CURRENT WEEK: edit weeks[0] in place.
TO ARCHIVE: keep old week objects, the loader renders them as additional tabs.

rankings.commentary: Three test sessions, three fastest laps. The paddock is whispering 'coronation' and BRETT isn't doing anything to dispel it. Untouchable on outright pace.
rankings.tweet:
🏁 POWER RANKINGS · WK 3

#1 (▲+2) $BRETT — 98.2

Three tests. Three fastest laps. Untouchable.

The paddock isn't whispering anymore. It's shouting.

#MEMEGP $BRETT

rankings.commentary: Lost the top spot but the long-run race pace is still ominous. DOGE wins races, BRETT wins qualifying. Sunday will be DOGE's day if strategy goes right.
rankings.tweet:
🏁 POWER RANKINGS · WK 3

#2 (▼-1) $DOGE — 91.4

Dropped the top slot but don't get it twisted: long-run pace still terrifies the grid.

DOGE wins races. BRETT wins qualifying.

#MEMEGP $DOGE

rankings.commentary: The biggest mover on the grid. Fresh upgrades, fresh tyres, and a driver suddenly looking dangerous in the high-speed sectors. Dark horse no more.
rankings.tweet:
🏁 POWER RANKINGS · WK 3

#3 (▲+5) $PENGU — 87.6

BIGGEST MOVER OF THE WEEK.

Fresh upgrades. Fresh tyres. Suddenly fast everywhere.

Watch this one.

#MEMEGP $PENGU

rankings.commentary: Pit Ops upgrade from 5 to 7 quietly transforms TURBO from a midfield team into a strategic threat. The undercut is now their weapon. Hold for now, rising.
rankings.tweet:
🏁 POWER RANKINGS · WK 3

#4 (—) $TURBO — 82.9

Pit Ops 5→7. The undercut is now a weapon.

Holding station this week but the trajectory is up. @AiTurboRacing playing the long game.

#MEMEGP $TURBO

rankings.commentary: Engine 7→8 lights up the speed traps but the chassis hasn't quite caught up. Dangerous in a straight line, vulnerable through the high-speed corners.
rankings.tweet:
🏁 POWER RANKINGS · WK 3

#5 (▲+1) $BONK — 78.4

Engine 7→8 makes them lethal on the straights.

Now they need the chassis to keep up. One-trick podium contender or full title threat?

#MEMEGP $BONK

rankings.commentary: Slipping. Other teams have upgraded harder this week and MASK's stat profile is starting to look pre-season. Time for a response from the engineering room.
rankings.tweet:
🏁 POWER RANKINGS · WK 3

#6 (▼-1) $MASK — 74.1

The grid keeps upgrading. MASK keeps standing still.

The engineering room needs an answer before Round 1 or this slide continues.

#MEMEGP $MASK

rankings.commentary: Inconsistent. The pace is there in flashes but WIF can't seem to stitch a clean weekend together. A driver issue or a setup issue? The paddock is split.
rankings.tweet:
🏁 POWER RANKINGS · WK 3

#7 (—) $WIF — 69.8

The pace is there. The weekends are not.

Driver problem or setup problem? @dogwifcoin needs to figure it out fast.

#MEMEGP $WIF

rankings.commentary: The week's biggest faller. SHIB looked dominant in pre-season but the rest of the grid has caught up and overtaken. Vulnerable in the midfield, scrapping for points.
rankings.tweet:
🏁 POWER RANKINGS · WK 3

#8 (▼-6) $SHIB — 64.3

BIGGEST FALLER OF THE WEEK.

Looked dominant in pre-season. Now scrapping in the midfield. The grid found a gear, $SHIB didn't.

#MEMEGP $SHIB

rankings.commentary: Chassis 9→8 is hurting. Cornering stability gone, lap times suffering, and the engineers haven't found the fix. Damage limitation mode until upgrades arrive.
rankings.tweet:
🏁 POWER RANKINGS · WK 3

#9 (—) $MOG — 58.7

Chassis 9→8. The cornering stability isn't coming back this week.

Damage limitation until the next upgrade window. @MogWorks need it badly.

#MEMEGP $MOG

rankings.commentary: New entry. FLOKI debuts at the back but the technical package looks stronger than the rookie position suggests. Don't write them off — surprises coming.
rankings.tweet:
🏁 POWER RANKINGS · WK 3

#10 (NEW) $FLOKI — 52.3

Welcome to the grid, @RealFlokiInu.

Debuts at the back but the package is stronger than the rank suggests. The Wolf isn't finished talking yet.

#MEMEGP $FLOKI
